questions.text: Which chamber of the heart receives oxygenated blood from the lungs?
questions.explanation: The left atrium receives oxygen-rich blood from pulmonary veins.

questions.text: Blood plasma is mainly composed of:
questions.explanation: Plasma is about 90% water and carries dissolved substances.

questions.text: The circulation that sends blood from the heart to the body and back is called:
questions.explanation: Systemic circulation moves blood between the heart and the rest of the body.
Arteries carry blood away from the heart, usually oxygenated.
Capillaries permit exchange of oxygen, carbon dioxide, and nutrients between blood and tissues.

questions.text: Lymph is a fluid that is similar to:
questions.explanation: Lymph is derived from tissue fluid and resembles plasma without red blood cells.
